content: We can reframe Tree of Thoughts (ToT) reasoning for large language models to use an Intrinsic Curiosity Module (ICM) that assigns intrinsic reward to exploring under-explored branches of the thought tree, instead of only pruning low-value paths based on pre-trained self-evaluation scores. This creates an autonomous, curiosity-driven ToT solver that actively seeks out unconventional reasoning paths it is uncertain about, rather than only following the most confident-seeming chains.
